Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant for vector search – 2-4 bit compression 86 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 116 upvotes, 15 comments
  3. DCJ11Hack+ – DEC PDP/11 based homebrew computer 17 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Fake Fans 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. A Taxonomy of Interiors 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 612 upvotes, 166 comments
  8. A Recipe for Steganogravy 130 upvotes, 29 comments
  9. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 95 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. iNaturalist 301 upvotes, 90 comments
  11. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 73 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth 389 upvotes, 157 comments
  13. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 284 upvotes, 111 comments
  14. The Hardest Document Extraction Problem in Insurance 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Show HN: TinyOS – A minimalist RTOS for Cortex-M written in C 37 upvotes, 9 comments
  16. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 203 upvotes, 86 comments
  17. Firm boosts H.264 streaming license fees from $100k up to staggering $4.5M 127 upvotes, 53 comments
  18. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 198 upvotes, 91 comments
  19. Oracle Files H-1B Visa Petitions Amid Mass Layoffs 286 upvotes, 143 comments
  20. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 388 upvotes, 212 comments
  21. Async Python Is Secretly Deterministic 54 upvotes, 23 comments
  22. Automatic Textbook Formalization 23 upvotes, 7 comments
  23. Iran Strikes Leave Amazon Availability Zones "Hard Down" in Bahrain and Dubai 85 upvotes, 43 comments
  24. How to Make a Sliding, Self-Locking, and Predator-Proof Chicken Coop Door (2020) 62 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 28 upvotes, 12 comments
  26. Show HN: Ismcpdead.com – Live dashboard tracking MCP adoption and sentiment 20 upvotes, 16 comments
  27. Age verification on Systemd and Flatpak 49 upvotes, 53 comments
  28. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 319 upvotes, 742 comments
  29. Why are we still using Markdown? 33 upvotes, 91 comments
  30. Charge Robotics (YC S21) Is Hiring Software and Hardware Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 7:30am, 4 April 2026. Source here.
